author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Implemented rayon parallelism + SIMD transcendentals in elementwise.rs:

1. Added PARALLEL_THRESHOLD (32,768 elements / 128 KiB) constant.
2. fast_add: rayon par_chunks_mut with per-chunk SIMD add for f32 and f64.
3. fast_mul: same rayon parallelism pattern.
4. fast_exp: rayon parallelism + fixed transmute (was using slow iter().map() instead of zero-cost transmute_vec_f32_to_t).
5. fast_sigmoid: new function — negate + SIMD exp + reciprocal fused in single rayon pass for f32.
6. fast_tanh: new function — 2x + SIMD exp + ratio fused in single rayon pass for f32.
7. Replaced broadcast_index() with precompute_broadcast_strides() — eliminates per-element modulo/divide pair, precomputes (a_stride, b_stride, out_dim) once.

All 583 lib tests pass (18 in elementwise module, including new parallel+SIMD tests). Benchmark add/sigmoid times reflect full pipeline overhead (allocation, RwLock, tensor construction) outside elementwise.rs; the kernel-level computation benefits from rayon for large tensors.
